I was a victim four years ago, and I’m so thankful for your videos Schlep. The girls’ stories you portrayed reminded me of myself. What happened to me took place off of Roblox four years ago which really shows how long and ongoing this issue has been. The girl you mentioned whose profile bio was changed to “<3 Matt”  really struck me because I did the same thing. I changed my bio to my predator’s name with heart emojis before my parents stepped in and saved me. I’ve never opened Roblox again since.
I just want to encourage anyone reading this to take this issue seriously and inform parents about how real and dangerous these situations can be, especially on platforms like Roblox.

author: @truthhurts69
text:
.....Walyand, MI. 20 mins from my house. I feel so sick. I hate and love these videos. I was assaulted as a child, my husband was too. We found out my husbands brother assaulted our daughter.... she is in therapy, doing so well now. We loved him very much, and I didnt hesitate to call the police in the first hour after finding out. It has been the longest year of our life, waiting for justice while the system that is supposed to avenge us just takes their time. Innocent until proven guilty is a double edged sword. He spent 8 months in jail, then was granted a bond reduction from 1 million, down to 100k. He is on house arrest, miles away from us. 
He was the only man we truly trusted with our daughter. We thought he shared the same hatred for peds because his and my husbands father is a registered S offender. Now, looking back, we cant remember any time that he vocalized that hatred with us. Though he did vocalize his hatred for their father. 

Brother's trial is coming up in November. He is facing up to 25 years for what he did. I offered a plea of 18-20 years if he does not force our daughter to come in and testify. I feel like im torturing myself with these videos.....please pray for us, and pray for actual justice. Because if he doesn't go to jail....I dont know how we will ever heal.

I am so thankful for the good men like this channel, Jdion, Alex and Skeeter. You all get us the justice we deserve. You are able to operate at a civilian level that police cannot. I love you guys. Thank you thank you.

author: @PawTisticCatLady
text: Jidion could literally sell ice to an eskimo. And watching him on his phone, genius. A lot of people, myself included, might feel too worried about confrontation or even feel too awkward to watch him on his phone, and that's important because he was trying to hide evidence. It seems like everything you and your team does is legit, and I really applaud you. There are so many "Vitaly"s out there, faking videos and/or beating people up and ruining any chance of prosecution. If their videos are real, they are doing more harm than good so I actually hope they're fake, I just struggle with accepting that someone would be willing to pretend to be a real "PDF file" and allow it to be posted on the internet for the world to see, for any amount of money. But you guys actually do everything so legit. The thing that worries me is thinking, if you were able to get enough evidence for all the people we are able to see you hold accountable, I can only imagine how many others are out there who you were not able to get enough info on to find them, or even worse, all the ones who don't get caught. It's a terrifying thought. What you guys are doing is God's work. These people should never have been able to exist, but all we can do is hold them accountable in any way we can, within the law and our own morality.

author: @yippppppeeeeeeeeeeeeee
text:
Objection!
Your Honor,
My client, known online as Schlep, acted not with malice, but with the sole intention of protecting children from real and present dangers. He is not a criminal he is a citizen who took initiative when he saw a system failing the very people it promised to protect.
Roblox claims his actions violated their terms of service. We contend that those actions, while unconventional, directly resulted in predators being identified, reported, and in some cases, apprehended. This was not for fame, money, or personal gain it was for safety.
To punish someone for safeguarding children sends a dangerous message: that protecting the vulnerable is a crime. We are here to show that my client’s work filled a gap in Roblox’s enforcement, and to argue that banning him and sending legal threats is not justice it is silencing a protector.
At the end of this case, I believe the evidence will show that Schlep’s intent, results, and moral standing outweigh any claim of technical violation.

Then the first NOT appropriate question he asked me, “what’s your age”, and I started getting bad vibes so I told him my actual age(16, and yes that was stupid of me). author: @Marsiscool-x7z
text: When I was 12 I was on pls donate running around and doing stuff then I met this bacon guy with a mask and duck plushie, he complimented my avatar and then we clicked. Soon I friended him and he asked me to join bedwards, we had lots of fun and he even showed me how to play the game! Which was sweet. At the end he sent me a message saying “You’re the best ❤❤❤“ (yes with the heart emojis) I didn’t think too much of it till I left. During that period of gaming I was multitasking doing a test online when I returned back to Roblox, it shook me a bit that this guy was still online. But not playing any games. Immediately I joined catalog avatar creator, he joined me as well. We exchanged pleasantries and then he asked me to join him in a different private server, He showed off his duck avatar he made and hey I love ducks! So I wanted to match. We messed with it for a while before I had to leave and go back to my other task. He was like “why?”  I was actually stunned. He even asked me WHERE I was going. I cleared up my doubts and said I was studying for exams before leaving. Then I joined a little while later, to my surprise again he was online but still not playing any games. That’s when I dug. I noticed that his friend count that I noticed that was originally up to 69 friends was now down to 1 friend. Which was me. It flattered me but also confused me. Either way I joined a game flicker and wasn’t suprised anymore when he joined me. When I met em. He said “he doesn’t like this game” and that let’s play something else. So I joined him in catalog avatar creator again. He made me wear a matching duck outfit and at the time I was too nice to refuse. After that I had to leave again.  The next time I joined again he was online not playing any games. Now I couldn’t join any of the games my other friends were playing as I was scared he’d come there and make a scene. After a few moments of this back and forth weird behaviour. He told me his age. And I had to tell him mine. I lied about my age at the time. Then he started dropping hints that he liked me. When I politely declined he tried bribing me with Robux and that he was a developer of a game and YouTuber. I was like “WE DONT EVEN KNOW EACH OTHER” so he forced me to join bedwars so we “get to know each other “ i joined then left, then immediately unfriended him. I was a bit guilty after this. But I’m glad I’m free :)

author: @MaruchiSan
text: Putting all of the blame for these interactions on the company is dishonest. There is NO safe space on the internet. Roblox has parental controls that completely disable in game chat all together. I understand this interaction moved to discord, but it wouldn’t have happened in the first place, theoretically if chat was disabled in the case this was an actual child. You can’t expect discord to moderate all private conversations. While I understand parents can’t monitor their kids online interactions 100% of the time, there are definitely tools and parental controls at their disposal and ABOVE all, EDUCATE your children and be open with them about what is and isn’t okay in adult behavior, make them feel seen and safe, don’t blame them, don’t helicopter them, and you’ll probably do a better job at keeping them away from predators than most parents.

author: @tatianna7angels
text:
That's why my older sister used to play with me or keeping an eye on everything i did online until i was old enough (when entered highschool and all) so i don't meet these peoples, unfortunatly younger I did meet some creeps on Roblox, Discord and all. But thank god i am now old enough to dirrectly block, report, ignore peoples or simply leave the games if i am not feeling comfortable, and I do the same things my older sister use to do for me.. to my nephews. Because, after all.. Roblox was a game to have fun and not being targeted by predators. We can't keep kids from a game that looks fun to them or they are going to try and be sneaky behind our back to play it. So, parents, aunts, uncles, grands-parents, older siblings.. anyone adults! just keep an eye on what the kids are doing. It's not about the kids having a personal life, or being like "they can do whatever they want, they are old enough" when they are 9 or 13 years old. It's terrifying to think that someone who approach your kid ONLINE, can Try socializing with the child to get more informations from them and meet them irl, or even stalk them who knows? and at that point you don't know these creeps or what they will do next, nobody wanna know. And that's why i'm a really stiffing Aunt, when it comes to the internet for my siblings' babies.

These guys are doing a wonderful job by working hard on those videos i respect that.

(And if my english is really bad, i apologize to anyone who did not understand the meanings behind my comment, English is not my primary language but i tried my best not using google translator.)

author: @beee923
text:
I love playing Roblox. I’m a girl, duh, and I dress like one on Roblox, too. It’s disgusting how many times I’ll be minding my own business in a game and people will come up and start harassing me, simply for my avatar. It gets to the point to where they don’t even phase me anymore, that’s how horrible it is. And that’s just the tip of the iceberg. You tell them to stop or leave you alone but it’s like they just get some dopamine boost from you being annoyed. They don’t even know what age you are. What if the person they were doing that to was 10? 12? 18? They don’t know. I know they might do it as a ‘joke’ but how far can a joke go before it’s just harassment? It’s even worse when you’re with friends, OR FAMILY. Like my god it is not hard to control your impulses. It has gotten so bad that I make my avatar ‘ugly’ (but still somewhat feminine) so I don’t get harassed simply for just existing. It’s not just female avatars it can happen to, it happens to anyone. Still, no matter who, it’s disgusting and suggestive behavior that should just not be promoted as a joke. Makes you wonder what those types of people do behind the scenes. Idc if they’re just ‘trolls’, are they all really just ‘trolls’?

You guys are absolute legends for putting a stop to this disgusting madness, despite how much of a mental toll it must take on you. Even if you don’t catch all the predators, you definitely discourage this behavior. Thank you.
(Also it’s lowk crazy seeing how good you are at making these predators comfortable with you all to make them drop even more info)

She answered something like, "I can't. My mom checks my phone all the time."
He replied, "How about you join my game?"

They went around, asking people to check their account, lying that they post demon slayer art. After a while, we grouped around this person, 3 strangers, my partner and me. They kept asking people, including a girl who later became our friend. And so, i got mad and curious, so i went and checked the account to make sure that theres nothing weird on it, that might scare or disturb little kids.
I went on the account. Guess what i saw? A D*CK PICTURE. I quickly went back and literally told the whole server that DO NOT check it and i got the guy banned. We laughed it off after with the others but im still SHOOK to this day. 😭

author: @watermonkes-m
text:
Thats cool and stuff but i think that plutonium is coolerPlutonium is a radioactive element, primarily used as fuel in nuclear reactors and for nuclear weapons. It’s heavy and unstable, and it undergoes radioactive decay, which releases energy in the form of radiation.

Here's how plutonium works in different contexts:

1. Nuclear Reactors:

Plutonium-239, the most commonly used isotope in reactors, can be bred from uranium-238 through neutron capture. In reactors, when plutonium-239 is bombarded with neutrons, it undergoes fission, splitting into smaller atoms (like barium and krypton), which releases a large amount of energy in the form of heat. This heat is used to produce steam, which drives turbines to generate electricity.

Plutonium also plays a role in some advanced reactors by being used as a fuel that could potentially generate more plutonium than it consumes, which is known as a "breeder" reactor.



2. Nuclear Weapons:

In weapons, plutonium-239 is used because it has a very high probability of undergoing fission when hit by a neutron. In a nuclear explosion, plutonium undergoes a chain reaction where each fission event releases more neutrons, which causes further fission events, releasing an enormous amount of energy in the form of an explosion.



3. Radioactive Decay:

Plutonium is highly radioactive and decays into other elements, such as uranium, thorium, and radon, over time. The decay process releases radiation, which can be harmful to living organisms if not properly contained. The half-life of plutonium-239 is 24,100 years, meaning it takes that long for half of the plutonium to decay.




Because of its radioactivity, plutonium needs to be handled with extreme care, requiring specialized containment to prevent harmful exposure.

author: @harmonymelody2834
text:
(This is a controversial question and I am autistic, I’m just asking out of wanting to understand more..)
I’d like to start off by saying I am in no way saying these people are innocent, because what they are doing is very wrong, illegal, disgusting.. but I’d like to ask… “you were able to walk away anytime” maybe it’s because I try my best to see the good in people but.. the bad ones who NEED to be locked up would run.. the ones who realize their mistakes and get their wake up call would want to change and are all for change, but are then handed over to the police.. while yes they do have to pay for their crimes.. won’t they just feel frustrated when in jail..? I guess that is what you are trying to teach them, they should be frustrated with their decisions because it’s not right.. but if he chose to leave.. he wouldn’t have been talking to you guys, which means he would’ve gotten away… or would you chase after them? I’m just voicing my thoughts out there.. please don’t take this as me defending any of these people..

That being said! Please keep doing what you’re doing! Maybe roblox will finally put an end to all this..

author: @Lachryma_Music
text:
You guys have really inspired me to start a campaign for more internet safety. This stuff happens a lot and there is very low awareness for children. In order for these creeps to stop what they are doing I am going to try and start a campaign. Back in 2020 when I had just turned a teenager, I was unfortunately a victim to one of these people (everything that happened was online) and Police got involved. I wasn't in the right head-space to see what the other person was doing was wrong and I came off social media after the whole situation. I knew the guy for a week and the things he said weren't easy to deal with and I felt like I couldn't say anything to anyone, I am glad my parents checked my phone and got me out of the situation.  I am going to try and start something in the summer to bring these situations more into light. 

I want to make the world a better place Schlep.
